name: Nmap
description: A versatile network scanning tool used to discover hosts and services on a computer network. Essential for the initial phases of penetration testing to map out the attack surface.

examples.description: Perform a rapid scan of the top 1000 common ports without pinging the host (treat as online).
Execute a comprehensive TCP scan, enumerating service versions and running default scripts on open ports.
Scan a target to detect its operating system, guessing aggressively if a perfect match isn't found.

name: LinPEAS
description: An exhaustive privilege escalation checker for Linux/Unix environments. It actively searches for misconfigurations, weak permissions, stored credentials, and vulnerable software versions.

examples.description: Execute the script directly from a remote host without touching the disk (stealthy execution).
Run the script locally and output the results to a file for later offline review, preserving the color formatting.

name: BloodHound
description: An Active Directory visualization tool that uncovers hidden relationships and complex trust paths. Crucial for identifying the shortest path to domain admin privileges.

examples.description: Collect comprehensive domain data (users, groups, computers, ACLs) using the Python ingestor.
Start the Neo4j database console required to run the BloodHound GUI.
